title: PowerShell Event Logs
description: Learn to list, query, clear, write, and manage Windows event logs using Get-EventLog and Get-WinEvent.

tasks.prompt: List all classic event logs available on the system.
tasks.explanation: Get-EventLog -List shows Application, System, Security, and other classic logs.

tasks.prompt: Retrieve the 10 newest entries from the Application event log.
tasks.explanation: -Newest returns the most recent events from the specified log.

tasks.prompt: List all Windows event log names using Get-WinEvent.
tasks.explanation: Get-WinEvent accesses both classic and modern Windows event channels.

tasks.prompt: Retrieve the 5 most recent events from the System log using Get-WinEvent.
tasks.explanation: -MaxEvents limits how many records Get-WinEvent returns.

tasks.prompt: Retrieve the 20 newest Error entries from the Application log.
Clear all entries from the Application event log.
tasks.explanation: Clear-EventLog removes all records from a classic event log.

tasks.prompt: Write an Information event (ID 1000) to Application log from source 'MyApp' with message 'Started'.
tasks.hint: Use Write-EventLog with LogName, Source, EventId, EntryType, and Message.
tasks.explanation: Write-EventLog creates custom entries in classic Windows event logs.

tasks.prompt: Set the maximum size of the Application log to 64 MB.
tasks.explanation: Limit-EventLog configures retention and maximum size for event logs.
